expected_findings.summary: Passive K-1 loss from Synthetic Partners 43 LP must be suspended
expected_findings.detail: The K-1 is marked passive and has a loss. Per IRC §469 passive losses can only offset passive income. If there is no passive income to absorb the loss this year, it must be suspended on Form 8582 and carried forward.
Foreign tax paid exceeds the election threshold for claiming the credit without Form 1116. Form 1116 is required and the credit may be limited.

expected_findings.summary: PTET election can effectively uncap the SALT deduction on K-1 income
expected_findings.detail: IRS Notice 2020-75 blessed state-level PTET regimes as a way to pay state tax at the entity level, converting a capped SALT deduction into a federal ordinary business expense that passes through as lower K-1 income. Consider the PTET election with each state where the K-1 generates income.

A prior year state refund is only taxable to the extent it produced a federal tax benefit (the §111 tax benefit rule). If prior year was at the SALT cap, the refund is usually NOT taxable. Do the worksheet.

expected_findings.summary: Rowan has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Rowan is 37 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: Jordan has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Jordan is 37 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: AGI above Roth IRA phaseout — consider backdoor Roth
expected_findings.detail: AGI $487,900 is above the single Roth IRA phaseout ceiling ($165,000). Direct Roth contributions are not allowed. Consider the backdoor Roth strategy (nondeductible traditional IRA contribution + Roth conversion) if there is no pre-tax IRA balance to trigger pro-rata.
Rowan has contributed $14,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $9,500 can still be deferred before year end.
Jordan has contributed $6,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $17,500 can still be deferred before year end.

expected_findings.summary: Illinois PTET election available on pass-through income
expected_findings.detail: Illinois allows a pass-through entity to elect to pay state tax at the entity level, producing a federal ordinary business deduction that effectively uncaps the SALT cost on this K-1. The return has eligible pass-through income but the state slice is not flagged as having made the election.

expected_findings.summary: California PTET election available on pass-through income
expected_findings.detail: California allows a pass-through entity to elect to pay state tax at the entity level, producing a federal ordinary business deduction that effectively uncaps the SALT cost on this K-1. The return has eligible pass-through income but the state slice is not flagged as having made the election.

expected_findings.summary: Pass-through income allocated across 2+ PTET states
expected_findings.detail: Multiple states allow a PTET election but apportion the deduction differently. Consult the partnership's state apportionment schedule before electing — the federal benefit stacks per state.

expected_findings.summary: Large SALT deduction — run Form 6251 to check AMT exposure
expected_findings.detail: Total state + property tax is $25,200. SALT is added back for AMT under §56(b)(1), which can tip a high-income household into AMT even at TY2025 exemption levels.

expected_findings.summary: Compute AMT foreign tax credit separately on Form 1116
expected_findings.detail: At high income with foreign tax paid, AMT requires a parallel Form 1116 using AMT sourcing rules under §59(a). The regular FTC and AMT FTC are often different.

expected_findings.summary: Underpayment: need $69,300 withheld for 110% safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: Prior year AGI exceeded $150,000, so the §6654 safe harbor requires 110% of prior year total tax ($69,300) to be paid via withholding or quarterly estimates. Currently $58,300 is withheld; consider a Q4 estimated payment or increased withholding of $11,000 to avoid the §6654 penalty.

expected_findings.summary: Need $79,040 withheld to hit 90% current year safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: The alternative safe harbor is 90% of current year tax. This is usually only easier to hit than 110% prior year if income dropped.

Based on a shortfall of $11,000 and the TY2025 applicable interest rate of ~8%, the underpayment penalty is approximately $440. Consider the annualized income method (Schedule AI) if income was lumpy across quarters.

expected_findings.summary: Use Schedule AI to reduce §6654 penalty on lumpy income
expected_findings.detail: Income arrived unevenly (RSU vest, K-1 distribution, or both). Form 2210 Schedule AI lets you annualize quarterly income so early quarters with little income do not generate a penalty.
